title: Autonomous ML Stock Explosion Prediction System
summary: Built a fully autonomous stock market intelligence system that screens 1,500+ stocks daily using 97 technical indicators and XGBoost classification to predict single-day explosive gains of 20%+. The system operates zero-touch via 5 scheduled GitHub Actions workflows with self-improving mistake-weighted learning that retrains weekly on accumulated prediction errors.
details:
This project implements an end-to-end machine learning pipeline for identifying high-probability stock explosions before market open. The system combines real-time data collection from TradingView's screener API, technical indicator computation from yfinance 5-minute bars, XGBoost binary classification with gain regression, and continuous self-improvement through mistake-weighted learning. All orchestration runs headlessly via GitHub Actions on fixed schedules, with persistent state stored in Supabase PostgreSQL tables.

The technical approach centers on a 97-feature engineering pipeline computing momentum (RSI variants, Stochastic, Williams %R, TSI, CCI), trend (MACD family, ADX/DMI, Supertrend), moving averages (EMA/SMA/WMA/HMA at 6 timeframes), volatility (ATR, Bollinger Bands, Keltner Channel), and volume indicators (OBV, CMF, MFI) at 4 critical timepoints: T-1 open, T-1 close, market open, and market close. The XGBoost classifier uses time-based train/validation split (most recent 15% as validation) to prevent regime leakage, with configurable scale_pos_weight clamped between 0.5 and 3.0. Training data combines a base CSV dataset (1.5x weight initially, reduced to 1.0x after 1,800 T-1 rows accumulate), daily winner/non-winner collections with balanced class sampling (30% flat, 30% slight gain, 20% slight loss, 20% big loss), and critically, a mistake-learner module that injects past false positives at 3x weight and false negatives at 2x weight to target the model's specific blind spots.

Key engineering challenges included fixing false positive misclassification through intraday high relabeling (stocks hitting 15%+ intraday but closing outside top-N now correctly labeled as winners with 1.5x weight), resolving feature schema conflicts via hybrid prefix detection that maps TradingView short names to model long names through a unified column map, implementing bimodal probability distribution detection that switches to percentile-based signals when prediction std < 0.02, and building a self-improving screener filter system that derives optimal thresholds (min HV, min volume ratio, RSI range) from the last 90 days of winner statistics and commits them as learned_filters.json for next-morning application. The system also handles edge cases like narrow-band predictions by falling back to top-2-percentile classification as STRONG BUY signals.

The production system executes 5 automated workflows daily: 2:00 AM screens 1,500 stocks and generates top-50 predictions, 4:30 PM captures top-15 actual winners with full indicator snapshots, 5:00 PM collects 15 diverse negative examples, 10:30 PM computes accuracy metrics and updates learned filters, and Sunday 2:00 AM performs full model retraining from scratch using all 4 data sources. A Streamlit dashboard with custom Terminal Noir CSS theme provides three-tab analytics covering daily winners with price journey charts, ML predictions with confusion matrices and probability histograms, and strategy backtesting with cumulative P&L visualization. The entire pipeline operates zero-touch with state persistence across 9 Supabase tables totaling 50+ daily prediction records and 30 winner/non-winner training samples per day.
